daily_brief: The US-Israel-Iran conflict dominated global developments Tuesday, with explosions and airstrikes knocking out power in Tehran while President Trump threatened destruction of Iran's civilian infrastructure if no deal is reached [France 24, Globe and Mail, The Hindu]. Spain escalated European opposition to the war by closing its airspace to US aircraft involved in operations against Iran [BBC, Al Jazeera]. Energy markets remained strained as the Strait of Hormuz disruption continued affecting global supplies, with Asian nations increasingly turning to coal and Germany's far-right AfD gaining support by calling for renewed Russian energy imports [Financial Times, The Hindu]. In Israel, the Knesset passed two significant measures: a budget ensuring Netanyahu's government survives until fall elections, and a controversial law making death by hanging the default sentence for Palestinians convicted of deadly attacks—a measure condemned by European nations and rights groups as discriminatory [Le Monde, BBC, Globe and Mail]. Meanwhile, Myanmar's junta chief Min Aung Hlaing was elected vice-president by parliament, positioning him to become civilian president and legitimize his 2021 coup [France 24]. Ukraine's Zelensky revealed allies have urged him to scale back strikes on Russian energy infrastructure amid the global fuel crisis [BBC Europe].

top_stories.headline: Explosions and Airstrikes Knock Out Power in Tehran as Trump Threatens Iran's Civilian Infrastructure
bullets: Multiple explosions and airstrikes hit Tehran on Tuesday, causing power outages in parts of the Iranian capital
US President Trump threatened widespread destruction of Iran's energy resources and vital infrastructure, including desalination plants, if no deal is reached 'shortly'
A drone struck a Kuwaiti oil tanker in Dubai waters causing a fire; Iran struck a water and electrical plant in Kuwait and an Israeli oil refinery came under attack
Iran executed four men convicted of links to banned opposition group MEK since Monday, as the war with US and Israel continues

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Brief headline coverage of Tehran explosions

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Emphasized Trump's escalating threats and potential seizure of Kharg Island

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Provided comprehensive live updates including regional attacks and Iranian executions

sources.name: Al Jazeera
sources.framing_note: Focused on energy shortfall consequences for developing nations

top_stories.headline: Spain Closes Airspace to US Military Aircraft Involved in Iran Operations
bullets: Spain's Defence Minister announced closure of Spanish airspace to US planes involved in attacks on Iran, including denial of use of Morón and Rota military bases
Foreign Minister stated the decision aims to 'not do anything that could encourage an escalation in this war'
A White House official responded that US military is 'meeting or surpassing all of its goals' and 'does not need help from Spain or anyone else'
Trump has previously threatened a full trade embargo on Spain over its opposition to the war

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Balanced coverage including both Spanish government statements and White House response

sources.name: Al Jazeera
sources.framing_note: Brief video coverage framing Spain as 'rejecting' the US-Israeli war

top_stories.headline: Israel Passes Death Penalty Law for Palestinians Convicted of Deadly Attacks
bullets: Israeli Knesset approved law making death by hanging the default sentence for Palestinians convicted of deadly terror attacks, passing 62-48 with Netanyahu voting in favor
The law applies to those convicted in military courts of killings intended to 'negate the existence of Israel'—language that effectively excludes Jewish citizens
Executions must occur within 90 days with possible 180-day postponement; law does not apply retroactively to current prisoners including October 7 attackers
European nations warned the law risks undermining 'democratic principles'; rights groups condemned it as creating 'racialized capital punishment'

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Emphasized practical impossibility of law applying to Jewish Israelis

sources.name: Le Monde
sources.framing_note: Highlighted far-right religious movement's role and characterized it as undermining rule of law

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Noted international condemnation and that legal experts say law 'effectively confines' death penalty to Palestinians

top_stories.headline: Israeli Parliament Passes Budget, Ensuring Netanyahu Government Survives Until Fall Elections
bullets: Knesset passed annual budget in overnight session before April 1 deadline, avoiding trigger for early elections
Opposition slammed budget for increasing funding to ultra-Orthodox communities during wartime; opposition leader Lapid called it 'the greatest theft in the state's history'
A last-minute amendment added $250 million to ultra-Orthodox schools, intensifying anger over community's military service exemptions
Netanyahu retains right to call early elections before the fall term ends in late October

sources.name: Le Monde
sources.framing_note: Focused on opposition criticism and ultra-Orthodox funding controversy

top_stories.headline: Myanmar Junta Chief Min Aung Hlaing Elected Vice-President, Moving Toward Civilian Leadership
bullets: Min Aung Hlaing received 247 of 260 votes in lower house election for vice-president position
The upper house elected Nan Ni Ni Aye from military-aligned Union Solidarity and Development Party as second vice-president; military will choose third
Parliamentary vote to select which vice-president becomes president expected later this week
Min Aung Hlaing has led Myanmar since 2021 coup that ousted Aung San Suu Kyi's elected government and triggered ongoing civil war

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Straightforward coverage noting process allows junta chief to 'exchange his uniform for civilian clothes'

additional_stories.headline: Asian countries increasingly turn to coal as Iran war disrupts Gulf gas supplies, with policymakers saying immediate energy needs override environmental concerns
Germany's AfD consolidates position as second-largest party in state elections, with energy prices driving calls to resume Russian gas imports
Ukraine's Zelensky says allies have asked him to scale back attacks on Russian energy infrastructure amid global fuel crisis
Satellite imagery confirms Ukraine struck key Russian Baltic Sea oil export terminals at Ust-Luga and Primorsk, halting shipments for first time since 2022 invasion
Australia investigates Facebook, TikTok and YouTube for possible breaches of under-16 social media ban three months after landmark laws took effect
Céline Dion announces 10-night Paris comeback residency for September-October, four years after Stiff Person Syndrome diagnosis ended touring
Former Nepal Prime Minister K.P. Sharma Oli remains in judicial custody as Supreme Court refuses interim relief over detention related to Gen Z protest crackdown
Amnesty International warns certain fans face 'serious risks' from US immigration enforcement during 2026 World Cup
Thieves steal paintings by Renoir, Cézanne and Matisse worth millions from museum near Parma, Italy in three-minute heist
Magnitude 7.3 earthquake strikes 35km northeast of Luganville, Vanuatu at 115km depth; no tsunami warning issued
